raw_consumer_statement:
Problem Description I am disputing five post-rental charges totaling {$10000.00} from XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX ( XXXX ). At the time of rental, I purchased the merchants XXXX  insurance, and the vehicle was returned after hours as instructed. 

After the rental concluded, the merchant charged my American Express card using fragmented transactions. To support these charges, the merchant submitted internally contradictory documentation. 

Specifically, the merchant provided a Letter Explanation stating that I left the vehicle in the facilities without notifying the damage. At the same time, the merchant also submitted a Damage Acknowledgment form bearing a signature they attribute to me. 

These two assertions can not both be true. If I was not present and did not report damage, it would have been impossible for me to sign a detailed damage acknowledgment at return. The merchants own submissions are mutually exclusive and call into question the authenticity and reliability of the documentation used to justify these charges. 

Concise Statement of Issue with American Express I attempted to resolve this matter through American Expresss dispute process. However, American Expresss automated systems have blocked further investigation on two of the five related charges, and representatives stated they are unable to reopen closed cases despite the submission of new evidence showing merchant misrepresentation and internal inconsistency. 

As a result, American Express has failed to conduct a reasonable billing-error investigation under the Fair Credit Billing Act, instead relying on procedural or system limitations while declining to review the substance of the dispute.
